response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and distributed-systems developer with genuine depth in TypeScript, Node.js, and software craftsmanship, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position mandates 8 years of C++ programming as a minimum qualifier — a language absent from his skills section and touched only incidentally during a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project. Beyond that critical gap, his background is anchored in web and application-layer engineering rather than cloud compute platforms, hardware architecture, or large-scale infrastructure leadership, which are the central axes of this role.
strengths: Consistent application of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple production environments
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency design
Active educator and community contributor through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, indicating mentorship capacity
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent: the minimum requirement is 8 years, but his skills list omits C++ entirely and only incidental exposure appears in one 6-month role and a student thesis
No formal technical leadership of cloud compute or infrastructure platform teams; all senior/staff roles are individual-contributor or small-team scope, not platform-level ownership

Core language stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and domain expertise (web, application services, GIS) are orthogonal to cloud compute, storage, hardware architecture, and the infrastructure depth this role demands
